atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -139,6 +140,8 @@ pub(crate) struct CanonicalInMemoryStateInner<N: NodePrimitives> / @@ -192,6 +195,7 @@ impl<N: NodePrimitives> CanonicalInMemoryState<N> / @@ -216,6 +220,7 @@ impl<N: NodePrimitives> CanonicalInMemoryState<N>
atoms.semantic_claim: CanonicalInMemoryStateInner owns a blocking canonical-state notifier, and both construction paths initialize it so every CanonicalInMemoryState can store blocking canonical update handlers.

destinations.evidence: The destination port has the same field and initializes it in both observed CanonicalInMemoryState construction paths.
The destination dependency crate provides the notifier type and constructor used by the exact in_memory.rs field initialization.

atoms.notes: Import-only and semicolon-only hunks in this file are formatting noise and are not counted as atoms.

atoms.semantic_claim: CanonicalInMemoryState exposes an API to register blocking canonical-state handlers by forwarding each handler to the inner blocking notifier.

destinations.evidence: The destination port contains the same public subscription method with the same handler type and forwarding behavior.
The destination notifier stores the same handler type that in_memory.rs registers.

atoms.notes: Exact code exists in the destination port; broader trait/provider exposure is covered by neighboring files, not this file's source patch.

atoms.semantic_claim: When canonical state is notified, blocking handlers synchronously receive the committed Chain before the existing async canonical-state broadcast is sent.

destinations.evidence: The destination port preserves the same synchronous blocking notification before the async broadcast send.

The destination regression test asserts that the async receiver cannot observe the notification before the blocking handler runs, then observes the async notification after notify_canon_state returns.
The notifier implementation invokes registered handlers synchronously in the caller's control flow.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Register a blocking handler that checks whether a subscribed async receiver can already receive the canonical notification during handler execution; the test fails if the async broadcast is observable before the blocking handler completes.
atoms.notes: This atom is high risk because it adds a canonical-state call-ordering side effect, but the destination mapping is exact and has a regression test for the ordering.

summary: The in-memory canonical state blocking-notifier behavior is ported exactly into the v2.2.0 port, including a regression test for blocking-before-async notification ordering.
notes: Only non-noise behavioral changes were atomized; import reshuffling and semicolon-only formatting changes were excluded.
